title: staff — model relationships
description: Two owned entities. Staff wraps User (like Profile wraps User in the user module — 1:1 satellite with RESTRICT protecting the parent from premature delete). Coach satellites Staff (again 1:1 with RESTRICT — a Coach row must be archived before its Staff can be offboarded). No `application_id` on any row — cascades through `tenant_id → tenants.application_id`. No `organization_id` or `region_id` — cascades through `branch_id → branches.organization_id / branches.region_id`. Every outbound FK stays same-tenant per tenancy-columns.md §5.

internal.description: 1:1 coaching-profile satellite. RESTRICT so Staff cannot be soft-deleted while an active Coach row references it — the offboard flow (CascadeCoachStatusOnStaffOffboarding hook) archives the Coach first. Enforced by StaffObserver.deleting.
Inverse. Coach cascades every tenant / branch / organization / region attribution through this FK — Coach has no direct branch_id / organization_id / region_id.

Self-reference for the org chart (staff_org_chart entitlement — Enterprise). SET NULL on manager offboard so the reporting chain doesn't cascade-orphan; the manager's direct reports become unassigned until an admin reassigns.

outbound.description: Every Staff row is tenant-scoped per tenancy-columns.md §3.
Home branch. RESTRICT: branches cannot be hard-deleted while Staff rows reference them. Branch archive is blocked by PreventStaffOrphansOnBranchArchived hook when active Staff exist.

RESTRICT: a User row cannot be soft-deleted or archived while an active Staff record references it. The offboard flow must run first — Staff.employment_status → offboarded — then the User archive is unblocked (PreventUserArchiveWithActiveStaff hook). This is the primary invariant of the module: no orphan employment records, no zombie logins for offboarded staff.
Denormalised tenant_id — cascades through staff.tenant_id but explicit for query-plan + FK integrity. Every write goes through CoachObserver which refuses when coach.tenant_id != staff.tenant_id.

inbound.description: Downstream modules (Wave 3+) attach to Staff + Coach via the traits shipped here:
examples: teams::TeamMember (Wave 3) — polymorphic pivot. When member_type='coach', member_id references Coach.id. When member_type='staff' (e.g. assistant manager on a team), member_id references Staff.id.
attendance::ShiftAssignment (Wave 4) — staff_id references Staff.id for shift-planning.
attendance::ClockEntry (Wave 4) — staff_id references Staff.id for clock-in/clock-out records.
finance::PayrollLine (Wave 4) — staff_id references Staff.id; reads compensation_amount_cents + pay_frequency for the payroll export.
scheduling::SessionRating (Wave 4) — coach_id references Coach.id; feeds the AggregateCoachRatingsJob.
inbound.onDelete: RESTRICT by convention on domain FKs referencing staff.id / coaches.id — Staff and Coach offboarding cascade-checks live in StaffObserver.saving / CoachObserver.saving.

accessor_chain.description: The Staff and Coach models expose delegated accessors so consumers rarely reach through to the User + Profile chain manually. This keeps the PII cluster behind one boundary and lets the redactor pipeline (from data-classes.json) apply uniformly.

notes: Staff carries `application_id` via its User (via User.application_id) — never as a direct column. Enforced by tenancy-compliance-auditor per tenancy-columns.md §2 (only the 8 named rows carry application_id directly).
Coach cascades organization + region through Staff → Branch. `coach.organization_id` and `coach.region_id` DO NOT EXIST — they are forbidden columns per tenancy-columns.md §5.

Staff.branch_id is IMMUTABLE post-create IN Wave 2b. Wave 3+ ships a StaffBranchChanged event + observer path when the branch-transfer flow lands (rare — most staff have one home branch for their tenure).

Staff.user_id is IMMUTABLE post-create. You never repoint a Staff row at a different User — that's a compliance smell (looks like an audit-trail launder). The correct path is offboard the old + hire the new.

Coach.staff_id is IMMUTABLE post-create. A Staff row's coaching career belongs to one Coach row; if a Staff promotes from receptionist to assistant coach then to head coach, the promotions are updates on the existing Coach row (coach_started_at + coaching_level), not new Coach rows.

The Staff module DOES NOT ship a multi-branch pivot in Wave 2b. `staff_branches` (many-to-many for staff who serve multiple branches operationally, e.g. a floating HR admin) lands in Wave 3+. Until then, one home branch per Staff.
There is NO `Employee` model. Staff IS the employee record — the naming reflects the domain-agnostic scope (a volunteer or intern is Staff too).
